lightBloom.note: The sharp QRS complex spike should flash with bloom on each heartbeat — the moment of electrical discharge. Bloom should be brief (3 frames) then decay
Heart rate number at 64px should have a constant soft green glow — it is THE vital sign, always visible across the room
SpO2 at 99% should glow cyan — second most critical vital

chromaticAberration.note: None. Medical monitors are high-precision displays — no optical artifacts. Clean image is critical for this genre

filmGrain.note: Film grain is disabled. Correct — medical monitors are pristine. But add very subtle TEMPORAL noise (0.005 intensity) that changes per-frame — digital sensor noise, not film grain

scanLines.note: Spacing 2 at 0.03 opacity, non-moving. Add subtle LCD pixel-grid effect: a faint grid of 1px lines at 3px spacing, opacity 0.02 — these are clinical LCD panels, not CRTs

vignette.note: 0.25 intensity is appropriately subtle. Medical monitors have even illumination. Push radius out to 0.95 — barely perceptible darkening

reflectionGlare.note: CT imaging panel should have a faint circular reflection of the overhead surgical lamp — the OR has intense focused lighting
Each panel should have a thin bright line along its top edge — overhead LED light bar reflecting off the monitor bezels

cameraDrift.note: Near-zero drift — surgical monitors are wall-mounted, rock-solid. Barely perceptible vibration from hospital HVAC

dramaticReveals.note: Fade-in at frames 0-30 is specified. Enhance: monitors should power on in sequence left-to-right with 3-frame stagger, each showing a brief white flash then their content. ECG flatlines during boot then starts beating at frame 20
On start, the timeline should rapidly wipe from left showing completed phases in 0.5s, then slow down as it reaches the current RESECTION phase

breathingAnimations.note: ECG baseline should drift up and down with a 14/min respiratory cycle — real ECGs show respiratory sinus arrhythmia. This is a surgeon-level detail that sells authenticity

Joint rotation at amplitude 5 is specified. Add continuous tremor suppression visualization — tiny 1px micro-oscillation that the system actively cancels (ghost outline showing raw input, solid line showing filtered output)

microInteractions.note: When Propofol rate changes by 0.1%, the digit should roll like an odometer, not snap — smooth mechanical counter feel
BIS should drift naturally between 40-46 — anesthesia depth is never perfectly stable

transitionMoments.description: BIS shift moment — BIS drifts from 42 to 45 (lightening). Propofol rate subtly increases from 150 to 155 mcg/kg/min in response. ECG HR ticks up from 72 to 74. Alert stack receives warning about EtCO2 trending up. Anesthesiologist (Hub) adjusts ventilator RR. This cascade shows the TEAM responding to a subtle change

dataDensityGaps.fix: Add WORKSPACE UTILIZATION indicator — percentage of reachable workspace being used, and COLLISION PROXIMITY warning distance between nearest arm tips
Add measurement calipers overlay — two dotted lines with distance readout (e.g., '3.2cm MARGIN'). Add SERIES/IMAGE NUMBER: 'Ser 4 / Img 142/256'
Add ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS mini-graph running along the bottom of the timeline — EBL correlates with surgical phases
Add COMPLIANCE readout (mL/cmH2O), RESISTANCE readout (cmH2O/L/s), and AUTO-PEEP indicator
Add TIME IN ROLE per team member and BREAK STATUS (has the surgeon been operating >2h without a break? Fatigue indicator)
Add patient demographics bar at the very top: 'DOE, JOHN M | MRN: 7284019 | DOB: 1978-04-12 | ALLERGY: PCN | BLOOD: O+'. This is ALWAYS visible on real OR monitors

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: Clinical cool-blue with high contrast — grade like a Michael Mann film (Collateral). Dark panels are blue-black (#0B1017 is perfect). Vital sign colors must be instantly distinguishable — the current palette (green/cyan/red/yellow/purple/teal) follows the real GE/Philips monitor convention. No changes needed to those

keyMoments.shift: Red overlay pulses across the entire composition at 0.05 opacity — the OR goes into crisis mode. All non-critical elements dim 30%
Brief full-white flash (1 frame, 0.08 opacity) when a phase completes — like a camera flash documenting the milestone

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: The 4 arm colors (green/cyan/orange/purple) are well-chosen. But they should appear more consistently — arm force gauges use them, but the CT imaging arm projection overlay should also use matching colors for all 4 arms, not just alpha
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Background is appropriately flat and clinical. Add a very subtle blue gradient across the top — overhead surgical lamp glow reflecting off the ceiling and back onto the monitors

typographyNotes.fonts: Inter is excellent for medical UI — clean, wide, high x-height for readability under stress. SF Mono for numeric readouts is the Apple medical device standard
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: SF Mono at weight 500 for vital numbers is perfect. Ensure proportional figures for labels but TABULAR figures for numeric values — digits must not jump around
typographyNotes.displayFont: Vital values at 64px are appropriately enormous. These must be readable from across the room. Add a subtle text-shadow (0 0 20px currentColor at 0.3 opacity) for glow effect
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: 9px timestamp labels are acceptable for non-critical secondary info. 10px data labels are fine. But alert text at 14px should be 16px with weight 700 — alerts must scream

continuousAmbience.sound: Soft cardiac monitor beep on each QRS — THE most recognizable sound in cinema medicine. Steady, reassuring. 72bpm = one beep every 0.833 seconds
Mechanical ventilator breathing cycle — inhale whoosh (14/min = every 4.3 seconds), brief pause, passive exhale sigh
Pulse ox tone — continuous pitch that tracks SpO2 level. At 99% it is a clear high tone. If it drops, the pitch drops proportionally — the most anxiety-inducing sound in medicine

eventSounds.sound: Double-tone alarm: two ascending notes (F4-Bb4), medium priority
Haptic feedback click — the surgeon feels this through the console handles
Satisfied single click — nurse confirms count correct

silenceMoments.description: CT scroll pause — the surgeon stops scrolling the CT images, studying the anatomy. All motion pauses for 4 frames. The room is still except for the heartbeat
